merger_description:
BGIS Pty Ltd (**BGIS**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in the Ascot Air Facilities Pty Ltd (**Ascot Air**) from its shareholders (the **Acquisition**).

BGIS is a national provider of integrated facilities management services and supplies heating, ventilation and air conditioning (**HVAC**) services largely as part of broader facilities management arrangements for portfolio customers, either through internal service capability or specialist subcontractors.

Ascot Air is a provider of commercial HVAC and mechanical services, supplying commercial HVAC design and construction, chilled water systems, refrigeration, ventilation ductwork, pipework and mechanical services, computer room air conditioning systems, building management systems and controls, drafting and design support and commissioning services.

Ascot Air provides services across South East Queensland, including Brisbane, Ipswich, Logan, Redland, Moreton Bay and the Gold Coast.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
BGIS Pty Ltd (BGIS) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Ascot Air
Facilities Pty Ltd (Ascot Air), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, BGIS, is a national provider of integrated facilities
management services and supplies heating, ventilation and air
conditioning (HVAC) services largely as part of broader facilities
management arrangements for portfolio customers, either through
internal service capability or specialist subcontractors.
The target, Ascot Air, is a provider of commercial HVAC and
mechanical services, supplying commercial HVAC design and
construction, chilled water systems, refrigeration, ventilation
ductwork, pipework and mechanical services, computer room air
conditioning systems, building management systems and controls,
drafting and design support and commissioning services. Ascot Air
provides services across South East Queensland, including Brisbane,
Ipswich, Logan, Redland, Moreton Bay and the Gold Coast.
Both parties supply commercial HVAC and related mechanical
services within South East Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between BGIS and Ascot
Air in the supply of commercial HVAC and related
mechanical services to areas within South East Queensland
b. while there is a potential vertical supply relationship between
the parties, there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or
other concerning vertical effects resulting from exclusionary
conduct post-acquisition
c. there are alternative suppliers of commercial HVAC and
related mechanical services in areas within South East
Queensland.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
